title: Research Directorates
text:
This directorate advances technologies to produce bio-based fuels, products, energy, and efficient transportation and mobility systems. Key research areas include biochemical conversion, thermochemical conversion, algal biofuels, and techno-economic and life cycle analyses. Transportation and mobility research accelerates the development of mobility technologies and strategies for both passenger and freight transportation across on-, off-, and non-road vehicles—including emerging technologies for aviation, rail, and maritime applications. The directorate is led by Associate Laboratory Director Adam Bratis.
Learn more about Bioenergy and Bioeconomy Research and Transportation and Mobility Research.
This directorate accelerates the transfer of laboratory technologies to the marketplace. It includes the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Center, the Technology Transfer office, and the partnership development team. All connect emerging advanced energy businesses with the financial community, NLR technical expertise and facilities, and the broader energy ecosystem. The directorate is led by Associate Laboratory Director Andrea Watson.
Learn more about working with NLR.
This directorate's capabilities span fundamental and applied R&D for next-generation energy technologies. Key program areas include solar energy conversion for electricity and fuels, materials discovery and development for advanced energy technologies, hydrogen production and storage, fuel cells, and computational science. The directorate is led by Associate Laboratory Director William Tumas.
Learn more about Materials, Chemical, and Computational Science.
The Strategic Energy Analysis and Decision Sciences directorate informs energy decisions through cross-disciplinary analysis, best-in-class tools and data, and technical assistance with partners. The directorate is led by Associate Laboratory Director Jaquelin Cochran.
Learn more about Energy Systems Analysis and Grid Modernization.
